title: Whole-Brain Deactivations Precede Uninduced Mind-Blanking Reports.

assets.abstract: Mind-blanking (MB) is termed as the inability to report our immediate-past mental content. In contrast to mental states with reportable content, such as mind-wandering or sensory perceptions, the neural correlates of MB started getting elucidated only recently. A notable particularity that pertains to MB studies is the way MB is instructed for reporting, like by deliberately asking participants to "empty their minds." Such instructions were shown to induce fMRI activations in frontal brain regio

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, default mode network, creative cognition, involuntary thought.
